UC-001.NAME: Bootstrap clean VPS with one command
UC-001.Goal: Deploy the full personal MTProto proxy stack on a clean Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 VPS using only `install.sh`.

Preconditions.Item: Administrator has sudo access on a clean VPS
Base domain points to the VPS
Administrator has a valid Resend API key

Flow.step-2: Install Docker and Docker Compose if missing.
Flow.step-4: Provision TLS certificates for apex and wildcard domains.
Flow.step-5: Build and run the Docker Compose stack.
Flow.step-6: Print the portal URL for the administrator.

SuccessCriteria.Item: State survives container restart through mounted volumes

UC-002.NAME: Request proxy by email
UC-002.Goal: Request access to a personal proxy by entering an email address on the web portal.

Flow.step-3: System validates input and creates a one-time verification token.
Flow.step-4: System sends a magic link through Resend.

SuccessCriteria.Item: Portal response and delivery email remain readable UTF-8 text for Russian-speaking users
Resend payload preserves readable UTF-8 subject, HTML, and text content end-to-end

UC-003.NAME: Verify email and issue proxy
UC-003.Goal: Verify ownership of the email address and receive a working `tg://proxy` link.

Flow.step-2: System validates and consumes the token.
Flow.step-3: System generates or loads a unique SNI subdomain for the user.
Flow.step-4: System persists the user record in DETS.

FailureCases.Item: Replay of an already consumed token
Persistence or proxy policy update failure

SuccessCriteria.Item: Issued `tg://proxy` credential is bound to the assigned SNI domain
Knowing the base secret alone is insufficient to authenticate without the issued derived credential

UC-004.NAME: Operate resiliently in production
UC-004.Goal: Run the service safely with understandable errors and OTP-style recovery behavior.

Constraints.Item: 443/tcp must serve both web traffic and MTProto domain fronting requirements
Errors from external systems must be visible and actionable
User-facing Russian copy must remain readable UTF-8 across email and `/verify` flows
Knowing base domain, port, and base secret must not be enough to use the proxy without an issued per-SNI credential
